title: pelargonium × elisae
intro: Pelargonium × elisae, a member of the Geraniaceae family, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between Pelargonium graveolens and Pelargonium fasciculatum, commonly known as the Rose Geranium. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of geraniol and other essential oils. The health benefits of Pelargonium × elisae include its ability to alleviate stress, promote relaxation, and soothe digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for anxiety and insomnia. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oil infusions, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
